message:
refactor(agent): unify message queue drain order, add compaction early-exit guard

src/agent.rs:
- Rewrite drain_steering_messages() and drain_follow_up_messages() to
  push fetched messages into the queue BEFORE popping, ensuring that
  fetched messages are deduped and ordered by the queue's internal logic
  rather than being blindly appended after. Previously, extend() would
  add fetched messages after already-popped local messages, bypassing
  any priority or dedup logic in the message queue.

src/compaction.rs:
- Add early-exit guard in prepare_compaction() that calls
  should_compact() BEFORE the expensive find_cut_point() scan. If the
  token count is below the compaction threshold, return None immediately
  instead of computing cut points, building retained message lists, and
  formatting the summary prompt — all of which would be discarded anyway.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
